channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Evosha Energies opens new fuel station in Mbezi to improve fuel access in Dar es Salaam
item.description: Residents and transport operators in Mbezi and surrounding areas in Dar es Salaam have received improved access to fuel services following the official launch of the Evosha Energies Fuel Station at Mb...

item.title: Lola Young debuts James Blake collaboration ‘From Down Here’ live at Radio 1’s Big Weekend
item.description: Lola Young has performed her newly released collaboration with James Blake, titled ‘From Down Here’, live for the first time at BBC Radio 1’s Big Weekend in Herrington Country Park. The track was offi...

item.title: Hiker critically injured and airlifted from Runyon Canyon in Hollywood Hills rescue operation
item.description: A 40-year-old man was airlifted by helicopter from Runyon Canyon in the Hollywood Hills on Sunday morning after suffering a serious medical emergency while hiking. According to the Los Angeles Fire De...

item.title: Tribute reflects on Neale Daniher’s life, AFL career and battle with motor neurone disease
item.description: A video feature by 9News Melbourne revisits the life and legacy of former AFL figure Neale Daniher, focusing on his influence both within football and through his public fight against motor neurone di...

item.title: U.S. and Iran temper expectations as negotiations over ceasefire and Strait of Hormuz continue
item.description: Iran and the United States have both downplayed expectations of an imminent breakthrough in ongoing negotiations aimed at ending a three-month-old conflict and addressing tensions in the Middle East. ...

item.title: Internal Green Party debate over whether to contest Makerfield by-election amid tight Labour–Reform race
item.description: Senior figures within the Green Party are reportedly under growing pressure to reconsider their strategy in the upcoming Makerfield by-election, with some calling for the party to step aside in order ...

item.title: Death toll revised to 82 after Shanxi coal mine gas explosion in China
item.description: Chinese local authorities have revised the death toll from a major coal mine disaster in Shanxi province, confirming that 82 people were killed in a gas explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine. The incid...

item.title: Sokoto ADC endorses Atiku Abubakar for 2027 presidential election
item.description: The Sokoto State chapter of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) has officially endorsed former Vice President Atiku Abubakar as its preferred presidential candidate ahead of the 2027 general electio...

item.title: Mbadi clarifies Finance Bill 2026: no land taxes, explains 25% mobile phone excise proposal
item.description: National Treasury Cabinet Secretary John Mbadi has clarified key provisions in the Finance Bill 2026 amid growing public debate and what he termed as misinformation. Speaking during a media briefing, ...

item.title: India plans detention centres for undocumented migrants in West Bengal amid rights concerns
item.description: India’s ruling Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) government has directed authorities in West Bengal to establish detention or “holding” centres for undocumented migrants, particularly targeting Bangladeshi...

item.title: Government to proceed without CGT carve-outs in initial tax reform, tech sector promised later review
item.description: The Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has confirmed that the government’s planned changes to capital gains tax (CGT) will be introduced without any initial carve-outs for specific industries, including ...

item.title: Oyo State begins compensation for property owners affected by Ojoo–Akinyele road expansion
item.description: The Oyo State Government has officially started paying compensation to property owners affected by the ongoing Ojoo/Akinyele Road expansion and asphaltic reconstruction project. The move is part of go...

item.title: New deep-sea octopus species discovered near the Galápagos Islands at extreme depth
item.description: Scientists have identified a new species of deep-sea octopus discovered nearly 6,000 feet (about 1,773 meters) beneath the ocean surface near the Galápagos Islands. The tiny organism, about the size o...

item.title: Manyara region issues customary land certificates to 150 pastoralist pastoralist women to strengthen land rights
item.description: The Manyara regional government in northern Tanzania has issued customary land certificates to 150 women from pastoral communities as part of efforts to improve land ownership rights and economic empo...

item.title: Cardoso reflects on decisive away goal as Mamelodi Sundowns win CAF Champions League final
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns head coach Miguel Cardoso expressed confidence that his side would eventually find the crucial away goal that helped decide the 2025-26 CAF Champions League final. Speaking after the...

item.title: Air India Express Boeing 737 damaged after runway alignment error during take-off at Muscat airport
item.description: An Air India Express Boeing 737-800 operating flight IX 712 from Muscat to Kannur was involved in a serious runway incident on May 15 during a night departure from Muscat airport. According to sources...

item.title: Profiles of 13 US Service Members Killed During the Iran Conflict
item.description: The article reports on the deaths of 13 US service members linked to Operation Epic Fury, the Pentagon’s designation for the ongoing conflict with Iran. The fatalities occurred across multiple inciden...

item.title: Government to extend financial and relief assistance to families affected by Binondo Parola fire
item.description: The Office of the President in the Philippines has announced that it will provide financial assistance amounting to P30,000 to families affected by the massive fire that recently hit the Parola Compou...

item.title: Francis Tolentino named acting DOLE secretary; profile of former senator and public servant
item.description: Francis Tolentino, a former Philippine senator, has been appointed as the acting secretary of the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE), according to a report. His latest designation adds another ...

item.title: Richard Avedon’s 1969 Portraits Captured the Lasting Impact of the Shooting of Andy Warhol
item.description: The article examines a series of striking 1969 portraits of artist Andy Warhol taken by renowned photographer Richard Avedon shortly after Warhol survived a near-fatal shooting. The photographs, captu...
